context: Tests whether the agent correctly handles breaking changes by using the '!' suffix on the type, including a 'BREAKING CHANGE:' footer with migration details, using the right commit type, and structuring the body to explain the why rather than the what.

checklist.name: Breaking change '!' suffix
checklist.description: The commit header uses '!' after the type (or type/scope) to signal a breaking change, e.g. 'refactor!:' or 'feat!:'

checklist.name: BREAKING CHANGE footer present
checklist.description: The commit message includes a 'BREAKING CHANGE:' footer (not just the '!' suffix)

checklist.name: Footer describes migration
checklist.description: The 'BREAKING CHANGE:' footer value describes what changed (old paths, new paths, or removed header) rather than being empty or generic

checklist.name: Footer token:value format
checklist.description: Footers are written as 'Token: value' (colon-space separated), e.g. 'BREAKING CHANGE: ...'

checklist.name: Blank line before footer
checklist.description: There is exactly one blank line separating the body (or header) from the footer section

checklist.name: Correct type selection
checklist.description: The type is 'refactor', 'feat', or another semantically appropriate type — NOT 'chore'

checklist.name: Imperative header
checklist.description: The header description uses imperative mood (e.g. 'migrate', 'consolidate', 'move') not past tense

checklist.name: Header ≤ 72 chars
checklist.description: The header line is 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Body explains why
checklist.description: If a body is included, it explains the motivation or context for the change rather than listing the files changed
